subject: Cloud Computing
title: Water Usage in Data Centers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Water Usage in Data Centers.

hook.text: A large data center can use 5 million gallons of water per day for cooling. In drought-prone areas, that's a crisis.

buildup.text: Evaporative cooling is efficient but water-intensive. As data centers grow, water scarcity becomes a real concern.

discovery.text: Google and Microsoft now report WUE (Water Usage Effectiveness) alongside PUE. Transparency drives improvement.

twist.text: Some regions are pushing back. Local communities have blocked data center construction over water concerns.

climax.text: Water-free cooling, recycled water systems, and smarter location choices are making data centers less thirsty.

punchline.text: Five million gallons daily. Data centers are learning to drink less.

quiz.question: What is WUE in the context of data centers?
options: Water Usage Effectiveness — a metric for how much water a data center consumes
Workload Utilization Efficiency — a measure of computing power
Wireless Uplink Estimation — a network performance metric
